action_choice_hints: Whether the action cell is in the same row as a moving token
Whether the action cell is in the same column as a moving token
Whether the action cell is the nearest empty cell in a given direction from a moving token
Whether the action cell is adjacent to a drawn trace
Whether the action cell is within K steps of an obstacle along a row or column
Whether the action cell is the first empty cell encountered along a direction from a control token
Whether the action cell is blocked by an obstacle before reaching the grid boundary
Whether the action cell is part of a contiguous segment of empty cells
Whether the action cell is in a region (e.g., top or bottom band) containing a specific token role
Whether there exists a path from a moving token to the action cell without crossing obstacles
Whether all cells between a moving token and the action cell are empty
Whether the action cell is aligned with a drawn trace in the same row or column
Whether the action cell is at the boundary of the grid
Whether the action cell is the only available cell in a direction from a control token
Whether the action cell is within a certain Manhattan distance from a moving token
transition_hints: Selecting an empty cell causes a moving token to advance to that cell
A drawn trace is created along the path between the previous and new positions of a moving token
Obstacles prevent movement and block trace creation
Selecting a cell in the same row or column as a moving token results in a straight-line transition
If a path is blocked by an obstacle, no movement or trace is created beyond the obstacle
The agent's position updates to the selected cell if the move is valid
Drawn traces persist after movement and affect future valid moves
Selecting a cell not aligned with a moving token results in no movement
Movement is only allowed through empty cells
Boundary cells restrict movement if no empty cell is available in the chosen direction
feature_family_hints: Directional scan features: to detect the first empty or obstacle cell along a row or column
Relative position features: to encode the offset between a moving token and candidate action cell
Contiguity features: to check if a segment of cells between two points is uninterrupted by obstacles
Alignment features: to determine if the action cell is aligned with a drawn trace or moving token
Adjacency features: to check if the action cell is adjacent to a drawn trace or obstacle
Path existence features: to determine if a valid path exists between two cells without crossing obstacles
Boundary proximity features: to encode whether a cell is at or near the grid edge
Region-based features: to capture whether a cell is in a specific band or area of the grid
Global count features: to represent the number of certain token roles present in the grid
Obstacle constraint features: to encode whether obstacles block movement or trace creation
Manhattan distance features: to measure proximity between tokens and candidate cells
Unique candidate features: to detect if a cell is the only available option in a direction
Persistence features: to represent the effect of persistent traces on future moves
Row/column scan features: to summarize the distribution of token roles along a line
Local neighborhood features: to capture the configuration of token roles around the action cell

diversity_requirements: Include both local (adjacency) and global (row/column scan) predicate templates
Require at least one template that combines path existence with obstacle constraints
placeholder_usage_limits: Limit templates to at most two free coordinate placeholders per feature
Restrict directional scan templates to a maximum of three directions per feature
avoidances: Avoid templates that only check for the presence of a single token role at a fixed location
Avoid templates that duplicate the same predicate schema with only minor parameter changes
